authority_boundary.reason: Direct incumbent evidence is now bounded more concretely, but Photon remains a semantics-mismatched challenger rather than a closed head-to-head runtime benchmark.

rows.label: ZPE-XR deterministic codec
rows.notes: Measured directly from the frozen benchmark workload in this workspace.
rows.semantics: Two hands, 26 joints per hand, quantized 3D joint positions with deterministic keyframe+delta transport.

rows.label: Raw OpenXR-like float stream
rows.semantics: Two hands, 26 joints per hand, position xyz plus quaternion xyzw as float32.

rows.label: Frozen modern comparator
rows.notes: Internal frozen modern proxy used in Phase 1.
rows.semantics: Two hands, half-float positional deltas and half-float rotations with zlib compression.

rows.label: Photon Fusion XR Hands (doc-derived compressed rotations)
rows.notes: Official Photon documentation states that one hand full bone rotations compress to 19 bytes instead of 386 bytes for full quaternion transfer.
rows.semantics: Hand-bone rotations only; shared local skeleton reconstructs visual hand state.

rows.label: Photon Fusion XR Hands (doc-derived full quaternion transfer)
rows.notes: Official Photon documentation states that one hand full bone rotations occupy 386 bytes.
rows.semantics: Hand-bone rotations only without Photon compression.

rows.label: Ultraleap VectorHand compressed encoding
rows.notes: Open-source Ultraleap VectorHand encoding fixes NUM_BYTES=86 per hand.
rows.semantics: Palm pose plus 25 hand-local joint positions per hand in an 86-byte compressed representation.

verdicts.claim: ZPE-XR remains clearly better than the frozen internal modern comparator on transport cost.
ZPE-XR currently beats the open-source Ultraleap VectorHand encoding on bytes/frame under the frozen two-hand stream semantics.
verdicts.evidence: doc/code-derived Ultraleap VectorHand NUM_BYTES=86 per hand vs measured ZPE avg bytes/frame

verdicts.claim: Photon-sized incumbent displacement is not closed because Photon's documented compressed path is narrower in semantics and smaller in bytes/frame.
verdicts.evidence: doc-derived Photon compressed rotations only = 19 bytes/hand, which is smaller than current ZPE bytes/frame but not apples-to-apples with the frozen full-position stream.
